field.label: Enable for EU Withdrawal Lookup Form
Enable Module
field.comment: Activates frontend and cron jobs. Default: disabled. Complete the manual setup checklist before enabling.

field.label: Request Number Prefix
field.comment: Optional prefix prepended to the withdrawal request increment ID in customer-facing UI (e.g. "RR-" renders as "RR-000000022"). Leave blank to show the plain padded number.

field.label: Frontend Route
field.comment: URL prefix used by the customer-facing withdrawal flow (e.g. withdraw-contract renders as /withdraw-contract/...). Lowercase letters, digits, dashes and underscores only.

group.label: Pre-Contract Information (Art. 6(1)(h))
group.comment: Mandatory pre-contractual disclosure of the right of withdrawal. Displayed on the checkout payment step. Each display is logged forensically (Art. 10 defence). Snapshot of the rendered text is stored immutably (Art. 6(5)). Counsel review recommended before customising any Annex I text.

field.label: Enable Pre-Contract Disclosure
field.comment: Master kill-switch. Disabled = block does not render and no display events are recorded. Disabling exposes the merchant to Art. 10 (12 month + 14 day extended withdrawal period). Default: enabled.

field.label: Return Address
field.comment: Where consumers send goods back. Leave empty to fall back to the store address from Stores → Configuration → General → General → Store Information.

field.label: Display In Checkout
field.comment: Off = block hidden but display events still recorded server-side via predispatch fallback. Use only for sandbox testing. Default: on.

group.label: Eligibility Rules (Art. 16 CRD Presets)
group.comment: These presets exclude specific product categories from the 14-day withdrawal right, per Art. 16 CRD. Each rule is OFF by default — enable only after confirming the corresponding product attribute is set correctly on your catalog.

field.label: Art. 16(c) Custom-Made Goods
field.comment: Excludes products made to customer specifications or clearly personalised (e.g. engraved jewellery, made-to-measure clothing). Requires product attribute is_custom_made = true. If enabled, orders containing such items cannot be withdrawn — customers will see this when attempting to start withdrawal.

field.label: Art. 16(d) Perishable Goods
field.comment: Excludes goods that can deteriorate or expire rapidly (fresh food, cut flowers, short-shelf-life pharmaceuticals). Requires product attribute is_perishable = true. Do not enable for all categories — apply it only to truly perishable SKUs, otherwise you will unlawfully deny valid withdrawals.

field.label: Art. 16(e) Sealed Hygiene / Health
field.comment: Excludes sealed hygiene or health items only if the seal has been broken after delivery (cosmetics, intimate apparel, sealed medical supplies). Requires product attribute is_sealed_hygiene = true. The exclusion depends on a seal-check at the time of withdrawal (workflow in phase-04) — a sealed, unopened return remains eligible.

field.label: Art. 16(i) Sealed Audio / Video / Software
field.comment: Excludes sealed audio recordings, video recordings, or sealed computer software only if the seal has been broken after delivery. Requires product attribute is_sealed_av = true. Same seal-check logic as Art. 16(e). Does not apply to streaming / digital-download content — use Art. 16(m) for those.

group.label: Detection (Art. 16(m))
group.comment: Art. 16(m) CRD digital-content detection. Determines what counts as digital content for eligibility purposes. The actual checkout-step waiver UI and email lives in the next section "Digital Waiver".

field.label: Enable Digital Content Detection
field.comment: Master switch for Art. 16(m) digital-content detection. When off, no product is recognised as digital, the checkout waiver step is not required, and the preset always allows withdrawal.

field.label: Detection Strategies
field.comment: Which product-type heuristics trigger the digital-content waiver gate. Downloadable + virtual are safe defaults; bundle_contains_digital inspects bundle children; custom_attribute reads the EAV flag configured below.

field.label: Custom Attribute Code
field.comment: EAV attribute code used only when Custom attribute flag is picked in Detection strategies above. Default: is_digital_content (boolean attribute, 1 = digital). Ignored otherwise.

group.label: Withdrawal Window
group.comment: Defines when the 14-day withdrawal right starts running (the delivery trigger, Art. 9(2)(b)) and how long it lasts in days (Art. 9(2)). Together these control the period each order's withdrawal is open.

field.label: Withdrawal Period (Days)
field.comment: Default 14 days per Art. 9(2). Extension allowed (e.g. 30, 100). Reduction below 14 is illegal — the field validates 14-3650 only.

field.label: Delivery Confirmation Statuses
field.comment: Pick one or more order statuses whose transition means the customer received the goods in your fulfillment workflow. The 14-day withdrawal window starts on the timestamp of the order's earliest transition into any of the selected statuses (Art. 9(2)(b) Directive 2011/83/EU). Multi-select supports merchants who use several "delivered"-type statuses (e.g. delivered + complete + delivered_partial). Until the order reaches any of them, the right of withdrawal is open with no fixed deadline.

group.label: Digital Waiver (Art. 16(m))
group.comment: Art. 16(m) CRD exclusion — digital content where the customer has (1) given express prior consent to begin performance and (2) acknowledged that they lose the withdrawal right. Two switches control this feature end-to-end: the Detection subgroup under Eligibility Rules decides what counts as digital (master switch); this section renders the 2-checkbox waiver UX at checkout and dispatches the Art. 11a(4) confirmation email. Both must be ON for the feature to work end-to-end. Per-locale text overrides below. Use this only for one-time digital purchases (ebooks, software installers, music files, games). Do not enable it for ongoing digital subscriptions or services (SaaS, streaming, hosted software) — EU case law (CJEU Sky, C-641/19) requires a different consent flow for those.

field.label: Enable Checkout Waiver Step
field.comment: When on, carts that contain a detected digital item render the 2-checkbox waiver step at checkout. Has no effect unless Eligibility Rules → Detection → Enable Digital Content Detection is also on.

field.label: Mark Virtual-Only Items as Performance-Started on Confirmation
field.comment: Virtual products have no download trigger. When on, the waiver confirmation email's dispatch counts as performance-start for virtual-only items.

field.label: Enforce Waiver on API / Headless Orders
field.comment: When on (default), digital-content orders placed via the REST/GraphQL API are blocked unless a genuine waiver consent record exists for each digital item — the same protection the storefront enforces. Turn this off only if you collect the Art. 16(m) consent through your own headless integration and write it via the waiver-event API; the storefront waiver step is unaffected either way.

group.label: Confirmation Email (Art. 11a(4))
group.comment: Durable-medium copy of the customer's Art. 16(m) waiver, sent immediately after checkout when a digital item is in cart. Required by Art. 11a(4) Directive 2011/83/EU — keeps a tamper-evident record of the legal text the customer accepted.

field.label: Enabled
field.comment: Disable only if you deliver the durable-medium confirmation through a different channel.

field.label: BCC Merchant
field.comment: Optional. Comma-separated. Blind-copies the waiver-confirmation email to a merchant audit address. Leave blank to skip.

group.label: Per-Locale Waiver Text Overrides
group.comment: Empty fields fall through to the locale baseline. Any override value becomes part of the SHA-256 hash stored per consent event — changing a field after customers have consented does not retroactively invalidate prior consents.

group.label: Frontend
group.comment: Customer-facing UX for the withdrawal flow. Subgroups: Buttons (legal-critical labels), Placements (where to surface the link), Return Reasons (per-item dropdown), Review Step (content on step 3 of the flow).

field.label: Step 1 Button Label
field.comment: ⚠ Legal warning. Art. 11a(2) CRD requires the literal text "withdraw from contract here" or a corresponding unambiguous formulation. The default value ("Withdraw from contract here") is the Directive's verbatim phrase; Per-locale translations are counsel-signed. Do not override unless you have received written advice from qualified consumer-law counsel in the target jurisdiction. Every override is recorded in the audit log.

field.label: Step 2 Button Label (Confirmation)
field.comment: ⚠ Legal warning. This is the label on the final confirmation button. Default ("Confirm withdrawal") is counsel-signed; Per-locale translations are counsel-signed. Override only with written legal advice; overrides are audit-logged.

field.label: Reasons
field.comment: List of reasons offered to the customer in the per-item dropdown. Code is the machine value (lowercase, snake_case, max 32 chars); Label is the visible text. Empty grid hides the dropdown entirely.

field.label: Enable "Other" with Free Text
field.comment: When enabled, an "Other" option appears in the dropdown that reveals a free-text field (max 500 chars).

field.label: Return Shipping Notice
field.comment: Text shown on the Review & Confirm step (step 3). Example: "You'll receive a prepaid shipping label in the confirmation email." or "Return shipping costs are paid by the customer." Leave blank to hide the row.

field.label: Return Policy URL
field.comment: Optional link rendered next to the shipping notice ("View return policy"). Accepts absolute URLs or relative paths like "return-policy".

group.label: Admin Notifications
group.comment: Internal alerts to your team. Independent of the per-email BCC Merchant checkboxes under Customer Notifications below — if you enable both, you will receive two emails per request.

group.label: 1. New Request Alert
group.comment: Sent the moment a customer completes the two-step withdrawal flow.

field.label: Recipient Email(s)
field.comment: Comma-separated. Each recipient receives a separate email rendered in their own admin locale (looked up via the admin user's interface_locale; fallback to general/locale/code). Invalid addresses dropped silently.

group.label: Customer Notifications
group.comment: All customer-facing emails sent by the module. Each email can be enabled/disabled, assigned a custom template (Marketing → Email Templates), and BCC'd to a merchant address. The shared Branding subgroup at the bottom controls the layout (USP strip, social links) used by every email below.

group.label: 1. Submission Confirmation Email
group.comment: Sent when a customer completes the withdrawal form.

field.label: BCC Merchant
field.comment: Comma-separated. Leave blank to disable.

group.label: 2. Approval Email
group.comment: Sent when an admin approves a withdrawal request.

field.label: BCC Merchant
field.comment: Comma-separated. Leave blank to disable.

group.label: 3. Denial Email
group.comment: Sent when an admin denies a withdrawal request.

field.label: BCC Merchant
field.comment: Comma-separated. Leave blank to disable.

group.label: 4. Cancellation Email (Admin-Initiated)
group.comment: Sent when an admin cancels a withdrawal request on the customer's behalf.

field.label: BCC Merchant
field.comment: Comma-separated. Leave blank to disable.

group.label: 5. Cancellation Email (Customer Self-Cancel)
group.comment: Sent when a customer cancels their own pending withdrawal.

field.label: BCC Merchant
field.comment: Comma-separated. Leave blank to disable.

group.label: 6. Receipt Email (Durable Medium — Art. 11a(4))
group.comment: Legally required durable-medium confirmation. Cannot be fully disabled (Art. 11a(4) Directive 2011/83/EU). Disabling here only stops the sender — the receipt is still generated and stored.

field.label: Enabled
field.comment: Disable only if you deliver the receipt through a different durable medium (postal mail, customer account download, etc.).

field.label: BCC Merchant
field.comment: Comma-separated.

field.label: Delivery Retry Attempts
field.comment: Number of delivery attempts before a receipt is flagged as undelivered.

field.label: DLQ Alert Recipient
field.comment: Notified when a receipt fails permanently. Falls back to Store Email Addresses → General Contact.

group.label: Branding (Shared by All Emails Above)
group.comment: Optional decoration of the customer-facing emails. Empty fields hide their respective rows. Brand wordmark, logo, support email, and store address are read from the standard Magento configuration tree (Stores → Configuration → General + Design → Transactional Emails).

field.label: Title
field.comment: e.g. "Free delivery"

field.label: Subtitle
field.comment: e.g. "EU orders above €50"

group.label: Social Links
group.comment: Optional URLs rendered as round monogram badges in the email footer. Leave blank to hide.

group.label: Privacy & Retention
group.comment: Two-stage GDPR data lifecycle. Withdrawal requests carry both personally identifiable data (customer name, email, IP, user agent, admin notes, denial reasons) and a content hash that is the Art. 11a(4) durable-medium legal proof. The two have different mandatory retention periods and are aged out separately.

field.label: PII Retention (days)
field.comment:
How long the personally identifiable fields (customer name, email, IP, user agent, denial reason, admin note) are kept before they are nullified by the daily privacy cron. The withdrawal record itself stays — only PII is wiped. Counted from the record's creation date.

Default: 730 days (2 years). This is the floor — GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) ("storage limitation") balanced against the statute of limitations for consumer-rights claims in most EU member states (1–2 years). Range: 730–3650 days.

field.label: Hash Retention (days)
field.comment:
How long the content hash (Art. 11a(4) durable-medium proof) is retained. After PII has been wiped, the record keeps only the hash and receipt timestamps until this period expires; then the record is deleted entirely.

Default: 2190 days (6 years). Matches the longest commercial-records retention obligation in EU member states (typically 6 years for accounting / VAT). Must be ≥ PII Retention. Range: 2190–3650 days.
